problem: If the three lines $3y-2x=1$, $x+2y=2$ and $4x-6y=5$ are drawn in the plane, how many points will lie at the intersection of at least two of the three lines?

classified_questions.question: What is the standard form of each of the given lines?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you identify the slope and y-intercept of each line?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are any of the given lines parallel to each other? How can you determine this?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we find the points of intersection between two lines algebraically?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Are there any lines among the three that are parallel? If so, how does that affect the number of intersection points?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Is it possible for all three lines to intersect at exactly one point? Why or why not?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we determine if two lines are identical or coincident?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you write the equations of the lines in slope-intercept form and compare the slopes to consider their intersection behavior?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How does the number of intersection points relate to the solution of the given system of equations?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you generalize this concept to a system of equations with more than three variables and equations?
classified_questions.label: e
